frameworks.name: global theories of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: broadcast-based accounts the book synthesizes with local theories

frameworks.name: local theories of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: region-specific accounts the book synthesizes with global theories

frameworks.name: signal detection theory
frameworks.one_line: framework underlying critique of perceptual-capacity null findings

frameworks.name: metacognition
frameworks.one_line: second-order judgments framing subjective experience mechanistically

core_question: What mechanistic account best explains subjective experience given current cognitive neuroscience evidence?
key_assumptions: subjective experience is dissociable from perceptual capacity
null findings in consciousness research can reflect low method sensitivity
physiology and AI offer convergent constraints on a mechanistic theory
key_findings: existing empirical studies are often misinterpreted as supporting capacity-based accounts
null findings are overemphasized despite limited methodological sensitivity
global and local theories can be combined into a single synthesis
interpretations: a synthesis of global and local theories better captures the qualitative nature of subjective experience
the resulting view extends to agency, emotion, rationality, culture, and clinical applications
